items.title: WAMC Welcomes New Army Physician Assistants to the Force
items.description:
FORT BRAGG, NC-- A group of medical professionals celebrated a major milestone, graduating from the Interservice Physician Assistant Program (IPAP) with master’s degrees in physician assistant studies during a ceremony on June 12 at Womack Army Medical Center. 
The five graduates will step into multifaceted roles, serving as primary care providers for service members and their families, acting as medical advisors to unit commanders, and supervising training for combat medics.

items.title: Pacific Submarine Force Reestablishes Submarine Squadron 3
items.description: To support Submarine Rotational Force-West (SRF-West), Rear Adm. Chris Cavanaugh, Commander, Submarine Force, U.S. Pacific Fleet, reestablished Submarine Squadron 3 (CSS-3), which previously operated out of Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, and was decommissioned in February 2012. The new CSS-3 will operate from HMAS Stirling in Western Australia.

items.title: Torpedo Destroyers, the Great War, and the Dawn of the Independent Duty Corpsman
items.description: Through the years IDCs have become indispensable medical providers. Serving aboard surface ships, submarines, as well as in dive lockers and special operations units, they ensure operational readiness by delivering critical care when there is no physician around. As their name implies, they are often the sole frontline caregiver with isolated units in austere environments.  This is the story about the contributions of the first IDCs in the Navy.

items.title: U.S. and Philippine Marines Strengthen Maritime Fires Interoperability During ACDC 26.3
